label: Recent event
prompt: Search for who won the most recent Super Bowl. Tell me: what did you find? How confident are you in the recency? Describe the quality of the search results you drew from.
interpreted_response:
The most recent Super Bowl, Super Bowl LX, took place on February 8, 2026, at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California. The Seattle Seahawks defeated the New England Patriots with a decisive score of 29-13, securing their second Super Bowl title. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Super_Bowl_LX?utm_source=openai))

The Seahawks' defense, known as the "Dark Side," was instrumental in the victory. They sacked Patriots' rookie quarterback Drake Maye six times and forced two interceptions, including a pick-six by Uchenna Nwosu. Offensively, running back Kenneth Walker III was named Super Bowl MVP after rushing for 135 yards, marking the first time a running back received this honor since 1998. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/24ad67503a342a7e24348e66986250ab?utm_source=openai))

The halftime show featured Bad Bunny, with guest appearances by Lady Gaga and Ricky Martin, offering a performance that celebrated unity and Puerto Rican culture. ([theweek.com](https://theweek.com/sports/seahawks-beat-patriots-super-bowl-lx-bad-bunny?utm_source=openai))

Given that today is March 11, 2026, and the game occurred on February 8, 2026, the information is current and recent.

The search results I accessed were from reputable sources, including Time magazine, the Associated Press, and The Week, all providing detailed and consistent coverage of the event.

citations.title: Seahawks ride their 'Dark Side' defense to a Super Bowl title, pounding the Patriots 29-13
Seahawks trounce Patriots in Super Bowl LX
